cards.title: Supplement Quick-Reference Dosing Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
SupplementStandard DoseTherapeutic DoseFormKey Notes
Vitamin D31000–2000 IU/day4000–10000 IU/day (deficiency)D3 (cholecalciferol)Always with K2 100µg if >2000IU. Check 25-OH-D target 100–150 nmol/L
Magnesium200–300mg/day400–600mg/dayGlycinate (sleep), Malate (energy), Citrate (constipation)RBC Mg more accurate than serum. Split doses. May cause loose stools at high doses.
Omega-3 (EPA+DHA)1g/day2–4g/day (inflammation/mood)Triglyceride form (not ethyl ester)Check omega-3 index. Thin blood at >3g — stop 2 weeks pre-surgery.
Zinc15–25mg/day30–50mg/day (therapeutic)Bisglycinate or picolinateAlways with food. Copper 1–2mg if >6 weeks zinc supplementation (Cu:Zn balance).
Iron (bisglycinate)15–25mg/day50–100mg/day (deficiency)Bisglycinate (best tolerated)Take with vitamin C, away from tea/coffee/Ca. Recheck ferritin in 3 months.
B12 (methylcobalamin)500µg/day1000–5000µg/dayMethylcobalamin sublingualNot cyanocobalamin. Sublingual bypasses intrinsic factor. Check holotranscobalamin.
5-MTHF (active folate)400–800µg/day1–5mg/day (MTHFR/high Hcy)5-methyltetrahydrofolateNOT folic acid in MTHFR. Avoid high-dose in active cancer without oncologist input.
Vitamin C500mg–1g/day2–4g/day (divided)Ascorbate (buffered for GI)Bowel tolerance dosing for high-dose. IV C needs clinical oversight.
CoQ10 (ubiquinol)100mg/day200–600mg/day (cardiac/statin)Ubiquinol (>40 yrs), ubiquinone (young)Mandatory with statins. Best absorbed with fat. Takes 4–6 weeks.
Probiotics5–10 billion CFU/day50–100 billion (dysbiosis)Multi-strain or condition-specificStrain-specific. Take with meal. Continue 4 weeks post-antibiotics.

cards.title: Herbal Dose Quick-Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
HerbStandard Extract DoseTincture (1:5)TeaKey Standardisation
Ashwagandha (KSM-66)300–600mg twice daily4–5ml twice daily—≥5% withanolides
Rhodiola rosea200–400mg/day (morning)2–4ml/day—3% rosavins + 1% salidroside
Turmeric / Curcumin500–2000mg/day (bioavailable)5–10ml 3× daily1 tsp powder + fat + pepperBCM-95, Meriva, or + piperine
Milk Thistle140–420mg silymarin/day4–6ml 3× dailySeeds crushed, steep 10 min70–80% silymarin
Valerian400–900mg at bedtime5–10ml at bedtime2–3g dried root, steep 10 min0.8% valerenic acid
Vitex (Chasteberry)20–40mg extract/day (morning)2–4ml/day (morning)—Agnuside 0.5%
Echinacea300mg 3× at onset (max 10d)4–5ml 3× dailyDecoction 2–3g rootAlkylamides (purpurea) or polysaccharides (angustifolia)
Ginkgo biloba120–240mg/day (divided)3–5ml twice daily—24% ginkgo flavonoids + 6% terpene lactones
St John's Wort300mg 3× daily4–6ml 3× daily2–4g herb, steep 10 min0.3% hypericin or 3–5% hyperforin
Berberine500mg 2–3× daily with meals——97%+ berberine HCl. Take with meals (GI tolerance). Rivalling metformin for T2DM.
Boswellia300–400mg 3× daily——65% boswellic acids; AKBA >30%
🟡 Tincture vs extract — quick conversion
1:5 tincture means 1g herb per 5ml liquid. A 1:1 fluid extract is 5× more concentrated. When converting: 300mg dry extract = approx 5ml 1:2 tincture = 10ml 1:5 tincture. Always clarify the ratio on the product. Most clinical trial evidence is based on standardised dry extracts — not tinctures. Use extracts when replicating trial doses.

cards.title: Nutrient–Drug Interactions — Quick Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
Nutrient/HerbDrugInteractionManagement
CalciumThyroid hormone, bisphosphonates, quinolones, tetracyclines↓ Drug absorptionSeparate by 2–4 hours
IronLevothyroxine, fluoroquinolones, levodopa↓ Drug absorption (chelation)Separate by ≥2 hours
MagnesiumBisphosphonates, antibiotics (quinolones)↓ Drug absorptionSeparate by 2 hours
Vitamin K2Warfarin↓ Anticoagulant effectLow consistent dose only; monitor INR
Vitamin E (>400IU)Warfarin, antiplatelet drugs↑ Bleeding riskLimit to <200IU with anticoagulants
ZincACE inhibitors, quinolones, penicillamine↓ Drug or zinc absorptionSeparate by 2 hours
St John's WortOCP, warfarin, SSRIs, HIV meds, many othersCYP3A4 induction → ↓ drug levels or ↑ serotoninAVOID with all narrow TI drugs
Omega-3 >3gWarfarin, antiplatelets↑ Bleeding riskReduce dose; stop 2 weeks pre-surgery
CoQ10WarfarinMay ↓ anticoagulation (structural similarity to Vit K)Monitor INR; consistent dosing
BerberineMetformin, CYP3A4/2D6 substratesAdditive hypoglycaemia; ↑ drug levelsMonitor BGL; reduce metformin dose if needed

cards.title: Paediatric, Geriatric & Pregnancy Dosing Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
PAEDIATRIC SUPPLEMENT DOSING
Young Rule: Child dose = Adult dose × Age / (Age + 12)
Always use lowest effective dose. Glycerite (glycerin-based) tinctures preferred over alcohol tinctures in children.
Supplement0–1yr1–3yr4–12yr12–18yr
Vitamin D3400 IU/day (from birth if breastfed)600 IU/day600–1000 IU/day1000–2000 IU/day
Omega-3 DHA100mg DHA/day150mg DHA/day200–300mg DHA/day250–500mg EPA+DHA/day
Iron (bisglycinate)1mg/kg/day7–10mg elemental/day10–15mg/dayAdult dose if deficient
Zinc2–3mg/day3–5mg/day5–10mg/day15–25mg/day
Probiotics1–5B CFU/day (L. rhamnosus GG for colic)5B CFU/day5–10B CFU/day10–20B CFU/day
GERIATRIC DOSING ADJUSTMENTS
Start Low, Go Slow
Renal clearance ↓ ~10mL/min per decade. CYP450 activity ↓ 20–40%. ↑ Fat:water ratio. ↓ Albumin. Increase doses every 2–4 weeks.
SupplementGeriatric AdjustmentReason
Vitamin D34000 IU/day (target 75–100 nmol/L)Skin synthesis ↓ 75%, reduced hydroxylation
Calcium1200mg/day in split doses max 500mg eachAbsorption ↓, bone loss accelerates
B121000mcg sublingual or IM monthlyAchlorhydria >50% of elderly — poor oral absorption
CoQ10Ubiquinol form 100–200mg/dayReduced conversion capacity in elderly
MagnesiumStart 200mg, increase to 400mgMore prone to diarrhoea. Accumulates in CKD.
PREGNANCY-SAFE SUPPLEMENT GUIDE
SupplementSafe?Dose
Methylfolate ★✅ Essential400–800mcg/day pre-conception to 12 weeks. 5mg if high risk.
Iron bisglycinate✅ If deficient27mg elemental/day. Test ferritin each trimester.
DHA (algae oil)✅ Recommended200–300mg DHA/day — critical 3rd trimester
Vitamin D3✅ Recommended2000–4000 IU/day
Ginger✅ Safe for NVP1g/day (250mg QID)
Vitamin A (retinol)⚠️ Caution<10,000 IU/day retinol. Beta-carotene unlimited — safe.
St John's Wort❌ ContraindicatedCYP450 inducer, fetal safety unknown
Kava, Blue cohosh, Pennyroyal❌ Absolutely avoidHepatotoxic / abortifacient / toxic

cards.title: Paediatric Supplement Dosing Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
Paediatric Dosing Principles
Children are NOT small adults. Weight-based dosing essential. Young's Rule: Child dose = Adult dose x Age/(Age+12). Clark's Rule: Child dose = Adult dose x Weight(kg)/70. Always use lowest effective dose and titrate up slowly.
SupplementInfant (0-1yr)Toddler (1-3yr)Child (4-12yr)Teen (12-18yr)
Vitamin D3400 IU/day from birth if breastfed600 IU/day600-1000 IU/day1000-2000 IU/day
Omega-3 DHA100mg DHA/day150mg DHA/day200-300mg DHA/day250-500mg EPA+DHA/day
Iron (bisglycinate)1mg/kg/day (premature: 2mg/kg)7-10mg elemental/day10-15mg elemental/dayAdult dose if deficient
Zinc2-3mg/day3-5mg/day5-10mg/day15-25mg/day
MagnesiumNot routinely needed40mg/day80-130mg/day240-360mg/day
Probiotics1-5B CFU/day (L.rhamnosus GG for colic)5B CFU/day5-10B CFU/day10-20B CFU/day
Vitamin C40-50mg/day15-25mg/day25-45mg/day65-75mg/day RDA. Therapeutic up to 500mg/day safe.
🟡 Herbal Dosing Safety in Children
Safe at child doses: chamomile (all ages as tea), ginger (2yrs+), echinacea (2yrs+), elderberry (1yr+), valerian (3yrs+). Never without specialist guidance: St John's Wort (under 12 — interactions and photosensitivity), kava (contraindicated under 18), licorice root long-term (hormonal effects). Use glycerites (glycerin-based tinctures) for children — avoid alcohol-based tinctures in young children. Calculate alcohol content before prescribing any tincture.

cards.title: Geriatric & Pregnancy Supplement Dosing
cards._legacyHtml:
GERIATRIC DOSING — START LOW, GO SLOW
Pharmacokinetic Changes in Elderly
Reduced renal clearance (GFR falls 10ml/min per decade after 40). Reduced hepatic metabolism (CYP450 down 20-40%). Reduced body water, increased body fat. Lower albumin (more free drug). Increase dose every 2-4 weeks.
SupplementStandard AdultGeriatric AdjustmentReason
Vitamin D32000 IU/day4000 IU/day (target 75-100 nmol/L)Skin synthesis down 75%, reduced hydroxylation capacity
Calcium1000mg/day1200mg/day in split dosesAbsorption decreases with age, bone loss accelerates
Magnesium400mg/dayStart 200mg, increase to 400mgMore prone to diarrhoea. Accumulates in CKD — monitor renal function.
CoQ10100mg/dayUbiquinol form 100-200mg/dayElderly have reduced conversion capacity — ubiquinol is the active form
B12500mcg/day oral1000mcg sublingual or IM monthlyAchlorhydria common in over-70s — poor oral absorption due to reduced intrinsic factor
Fish oil2g EPA+DHASame — check anticoagulant interactionsIncreased bleeding risk if on aspirin, warfarin, or NOAC
PREGNANCY-SAFE SUPPLEMENT GUIDE
SupplementSafe?DoseNotes
Folate (methylfolate)Essential400-800mcg/day pre-conception to 12 weeksUse methylfolate if MTHFR variant. 5mg if high risk (previous NTD, epilepsy meds).
Iron (bisglycinate)Essential if deficient27mg elemental/dayTest ferritin each trimester. Supplement if under 30 µg/L.
DHA (algae oil)Recommended200-300mg DHA/dayCritical 3rd trimester for fetal brain development. Algae oil preferred — no mercury risk.
Vitamin D3Recommended2000-4000 IU/dayTarget 25(OH)D above 75 nmol/L
Magnesium glycinateSafe300mg/dayReduces leg cramps, constipation, pre-eclampsia risk
GingerSafe (NVP)1g/day (250mg QID)Evidence-based for nausea. Safe throughout pregnancy.
Vitamin A (retinol)CautionUnder 10,000 IU retinol/day. Beta-carotene unlimited.Over 10,000 IU retinol = teratogenic risk (cardiac defects). Avoid cod liver oil.
St John's WortContraindicatedAvoid completelyCYP450 inducer, unknown fetal risk, avoid throughout pregnancy
